item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Given that this article is causing stars to literally vanish from the sky, it seems like that whole foundation project that is focused on extraterrestrials would be one of the primary backers to the Foundation playing a role in getting SCP-3909-A instances up to snuff. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

While it is unclear at this time whether SCP-3909-C poses a direct threat to Earth's solar system, SCP-3909 has been preemptively upgraded to Keter-class, and containment has been re-oriented to maximize the performance of SCP-3909-A. Since enactment of these containment procedures, star disappearance associated with SCP-3909-C has decreased by 69% over historical averages.

To this:

While it is unclear at this time whether SCP-3909-C poses a direct threat to Earth's solar system, per recommendation of Project Heimdall SCP-3909 has been preemptively upgraded to Keter-class, and containment has been re-oriented to maximize the performance of SCP-3909-A. Since enactment of these containment procedures, star disappearance associated with SCP-3909-C has decreased by 69% over historical averages.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: How the Foundation could have figured this out.
item.content:encoded:
1. When the first instance came back and reported what happened (probably online) it came to the Foundation's attention.
  2. They mentioned that "Bring the Ruckus" was conspicuously in English.
  3. The second and subsequent instances reported the same thing.
  4. The Foundation searched for that phrase and found the Wu-Tang track.
  5. They tried to correlate the candidates with the song and realized that none of the candidates are older than the song.
  6. They interviewed the candidates parents, and at least one set of them remembered they were listening to "Bring da Ruckus" when they conceived the champion.
  7. They could try to confirm it forensically by figuring out if many of the champions parents were listening to it via streaming or had recently bought the CD around nine months before the champion was born.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is obviously dorky, but it's got a lot of charm. The containment procedures are also some of the more interesting and unusual that I've seen in a while, providing an interesting, non-intrusive example of using SCPs to contain others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wu tang is for the children!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, staff noticed that and will keep an eye on said rando.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, it's obvious that the title is meant to be silly. Some people need to cool their juices.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… I just double-checked and someone changed the title on the mainlist to replace "Fuck" with "[Verb Redacted]". At least a day ago. That'd explain it.

Suffice to say that the opinion of whichever rando made that change does not reflect that of the site as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does he realize there's fuck in the title?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what are you talking about

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is fuk not allowed anymore? Since when did the horror forum about unnatural monsters and expending human lives supposed to be FCC- friendly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The parents of those who work for the foundation go to some weird-ass college parties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IMO, it's that contrast that makes it: the fact that the SCPF have no choice but to help people engage in dumb, trivial tasks for the amusement of aliens is brilliant. It's not too overpowered: there's the decrease in disappearances after all, but it's severe enough to ensure the Foundation's continied participation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm torn — it's fine the idea is dorky as hell, I prefer funny SCPs to ones that try to be scary, it's just, the consequences are too potentially dire for something so silly. no vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

SHOW ME WHAT YOU GOTTTTTTTTT. I WANT TO SEE WHAT YOU GOTTTTTT.

I actually enjoyed the stars disappearing bit. I thought it was a great way to raise the stakes dramatically while tying it into the whole interstellar bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lol

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, gonna echo ARD down here, but kick it up a notch to a staffpost.

Please do not say things like 'I don't mean to be racist BUUUUUUUUUUUT [racist thing]'.

That's still racist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't mean to sound like a racist stereotypical prick buuuuuuuuut ……… Their would be a lot of instances.

I'm not sure what I'm supposed to take away from this, because everything I do suggests that you do, in fact, sound like a racist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's Wu Tang clan…. Of course the foundation would find it if it were anomalous, If anything their are too few instances. I don't mean to sound like a racist stereotypical prick buuuuuuuuut ……… Their would be a lot of instances.

Also I think this works better as a -J sense it is written comedically…or that's my take away from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beyond Star Signals-level though. 36 Chambers has set the blueprint for the entirety of hardcore hip hop, has been sampled in numerous songs, referenced in lyrics to numerous songs, has had documentaries and behind-the-scenes about it, has sold millions of album copies (not to mention bootleg tapes of it and its demos circulating the music underground scene, as I found out first-hand last time I went to Brazil) and has been used in countless media. It would be the absolute highest-tier of a suppression job, because you're targeting something that's not really just a memory or an event. Its status is harder to quantify than just an album that sold very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same. Enjoyed most of it but the whole star thing felt so weridly out of place that it tamped me down to a no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not hung up on the album thing, although its status and popularity wouldn't be an impediment to suppression based on what goes on in other articles!

Does that honestly sound like it would fit in well with the article? Do you think that's the right tone to set for this subject matter?

Honestly, yes. I thought the idea of the Foundation increasing both the odds of spotting when contact is made with -As, and the chances of the -As winning the contest, would be sensible in light of the implied consequences of failure. And the idea of the Foundation trying to ensure that all its employees were conceived to "Bring da Ruckus" seemed to me to fit nicely with the overall goofy tone.

If my comment is sounding like some sort of eugenics argument, then that is not at all my intention! Happy to have my ignorance or insensitivity pointed out if necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the list of instances pretty entertaining, but the mass star disappearance felt like kind of a tacked-on, random, and pretty implausible way to raise the stakes. (It's possible I'm missing something from the song, I guess.) No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why does containment not involve taking the album out of circulation?

36 Chambers isn't some no-name record the Foundation can just look at and be like "welp this is anomalous pull it off the shelves boys". It changed the whole genre. It's sold millions of copies, not counting all the places it's been downloaded or ripped or stashed somewhere. Taking an album "out of circulation" is something you can do when it's new, not when it's over twenty years old.

Or better yet, a selective breeding and training program?

Does that honestly sound like it would fit in well with the article? Do you think that's the right tone to set for this subject matter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Too much of a stretch for me, I'm afraid.

Why does containment not involve taking the album out of circulation? Or better yet, a selective breeding and training program?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy whenever skips are discovered by the Foundation that would be so implausible for the Foundation to discover and yet the mystery of how they were discovered is never revealed. It makes these sort of things more enticing to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As interesting as it is, I can't see a realistic way that the Foundation could even discover the cause of this skip. Like, the scenarios in which this has to come in effect are so precise, so minute, that it would take a miracle for the Foundation to even encounter this happening naturally.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed.

And yes, it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's fun, I like it. Is "quote making" an intentional reference to the "euphoric" copypasta?

Also, in the addendum, I think you meant to put "was" before "discovered".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the beginning because of the Wu-Tang Clan but lost me because of how inured I am to aliens that are basically green-skinned humans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly though, I have to wonder how the hell the Foundation became aware of this at all.

Same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How dare you not fund a way to inckude Shaolin Shadow Boxing into this, immediate dislike!/s

More seriously, I can’t help but like this dorky little piece of work. It has a nice mix of Quirky, Absurd Humour and stakes being that makes what would probably be just an absurd concept actually work. The survivors accounts helped it as well (Even if the addition if an Alien rap battle felt a bit too on the nose).

Honestly though, I have to wonder how the hell the Foundation became aware of this at all. Like, sure, alien abductions and people wandering aound going “Oh what the fuck”, I can see that, but where did they get the correlation between Aliens and Brung Da Ruckus from?

item.title: h
item.content:encoded:
ave some goofy sci-fi i wrote

thanks to monkeysky for looking this over
